source_intake_record.raw_question_text: If a photon travels in an electric field， usually produced by a nucleus, such as \^{12}C, it can spontaneously disintegrate into an electron and a positron which is the “anti-particle” of the electron. Both electron and positron have mass m = 9.11 \times 10^{-31} kg. This process is called pair production. What is the recoil velocity of ^{12}C as a result of pair production?
